channel.title: The Olsen Tribune
channel.description: Daily business intelligence — markets, technology, healthcare, politics, and analysis.

item.title: Bank of England Deputy Breaks Protocol With Stark Warning on Stretched Equity Valuations
item.description: A rare public intervention from Threadneedle Street has reignited debate over whether global stock markets have run too far ahead of underlying fundamentals.

item.title: Crude's Stubborn Climb Forces Investors to Rewrite the Inflation Playbook
item.description: With diplomatic channels narrowing and supply chains under renewed strain, the oil complex is dictating a new set of winners and losers across global equity sectors.

item.title: Meta's Cosmic Gamble: Inside the Tech Giant's Bet on Solar Power Beamed From Orbit
item.description: A modest first contract with a startup called Overview Energy signals that hyperscalers are willing to fund science-fiction-grade infrastructure to feed their insatiable AI data centers.

item.title: When Bots Buy From Bots: Anthropic's Marketplace Experiment Hints at a New Economic Layer
item.description: A controlled trial in which AI agents transacted with one another exposes both the promise and the peril of automating the act of commerce itself.

item.title: Britain's Healthy Lifespan Shrinks as Housing and Obesity Crises Compound
item.description: A two-year drop in healthy life expectancy over the past decade is forcing policymakers to confront the structural drivers of preventable disease.

item.title: Gene Therapy's Quiet Commercial Breakthrough in Childhood Blindness
item.description: A six-year-old's restored vision underscores how one-time genetic treatments are reshaping pediatric medicine — and the economics of rare disease.

item.title: Gunfire at Correspondents' Dinner Reignites Debate Over Presidential Security Posture
item.description: The third major security incident involving President Trump in as many years has lawmakers and federal agencies questioning whether protective protocols have kept pace with a hardening threat environment.

item.title: Congress Returns to a Hardening DHS Shutdown and a Shrinking Window for Compromise
item.description: With border operations strained and tens of thousands of federal workers furloughed, lawmakers face mounting pressure to resolve a funding standoff that has now entered its fourth week.

item.title: The Long Tail of the Iran Conflict Is Now Sitting at the Kitchen Table
item.description: From jet fuel surcharges to grocery bills, the macroeconomic spillover from a stalled Middle East peace process is quietly rewriting household finances across the West.

item.title: Meta's Layoffs Reveal the Uncomfortable Math Behind the AI Capex Boom
item.description: As Big Tech pours unprecedented capital into artificial intelligence, the labor side of the ledger is being rewritten — and the entry-level rungs of the career ladder are disappearing first.

item.title: Bank of England Deputy Breaks Protocol With Blunt Warning on Stretched Equities
item.description: A rare public rebuke from Threadneedle Street's second-in-command rattles a market already grappling with war-driven inflation and faltering earnings momentum.

item.title: Iran Conflict Reshapes Global Trade Flows as Inflation Re-Accelerates
item.description: From British fuel pumps to Chinese factory floors, the Middle East war is rewriting the cost structure of the world economy and forcing policymakers to recalibrate.

item.title: Google's $40 Billion Anthropic Bet Redraws the AI Compute Map
item.description: The search giant's deepening commitment to a rival lab underscores how scarcity of advanced chips is reshaping the competitive logic of artificial intelligence.

item.title: After a Long Drought, Climate Tech Finds an Opening on Wall Street
item.description: X-energy's debut and Fervo's pending listing suggest public investors may finally be willing to underwrite the long, capital-intensive timelines of energy innovation.

item.title: Britain's NHS Faces a Workforce Paradox as Newly Qualified Clinicians Sit Idle
item.description: A hiring freeze across English trusts has left thousands of midwives, nurses and physiotherapists unable to secure roles, even as patient waiting lists balloon past historic highs.

item.title: Gene Therapy's Quiet Revolution Tests the Limits of Health-System Budgets
item.description: As one-time treatments restore sight, mobility and immune function in children with rare conditions, regulators and insurers are confronting pricing models the existing system was never built to absorb.

item.title: High Court Weighs Geofencing Case That Could Reshape Fourth Amendment for the Digital Age
item.description: Justices appear divided as a Virginia bank robbery investigation forces a reckoning over how police use sweeping location data dragnets.

item.title: White House Abruptly Pulls Envoys From Islamabad as Iran Diplomacy Collapses
item.description: President Trump's decision to cancel the trip led by Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner deepens uncertainty over a war that is approaching its constitutional reckoning in Congress.

item.title: The Eight-Month Shadow: How the Iran Conflict Is Rewiring Western Price Expectations
item.description: Government ministers now concede that elevated prices could persist well into next year, complicating central bank forecasts and household budgets alike.

item.title: Meta's Cull and the Vanishing Graduate Job: Silicon Valley's AI Bargain Comes Due
item.description: A ten percent workforce reduction at Meta lands as warnings mount that artificial intelligence is hollowing out the bottom rung of the white-collar career ladder.
